title: The Secret Code of Fake Moles (17th Century France)
script: In 17th century France, you didn't just tell someone you were single. You stuck a tiny piece of black velvet on your face. French aristocrats used fake beauty marks called mouches, and each placement was a secret code. Near the corner of your mouth? That meant I want to kiss you. Near your eye? Feeling flirtatious. On your cheek? I'm taken, back off. Both men and women did this. And here's the best part. Some aristocrats showed up to parties with six mouches plastered across their face, basically screaming I'm available at maximum volume. It was the Tinder profile of the 1600s.

scenes.prompt: Hyper-realistic portrait photography of a jaw-droppingly gorgeous 25-year-old French aristocratic woman with flawless porcelain skin, a single small black velvet beauty mark near the corner of her red lips, powdered platinum hair in an elaborate updo with pearl pins, wearing a pristine royal blue silk gown with gold embroidery and lace trim, standing in a gleaming Versailles-style palace hallway with polished marble floors and gilded walls. She gazes directly at the camera with a mysterious knowing smile. Vibrant colors, hyper-saturated, 8k resolution, cinematic lighting, documentary style realism, highly detailed, shot on 35mm lens, photorealistic. No painting.

A jaw-droppingly gorgeous 25-year-old French aristocratic woman with flawless porcelain skin, powdered platinum hair in an elaborate updo, wearing a pristine emerald green silk gown with gold embroidery and white lace trim. She holds a tiny piece of black velvet between her delicate fingers near her face, examining it in front of an ornate gilded mirror in a candle-lit palace dressing room. She places the velvet dot near the corner of her mouth and turns slightly toward the camera with a coy, seductive smile. Vibrant colors, hyper-saturated, warm golden candlelight, 8k resolution, cinematic lighting, dramatic shadows, documentary style realism, highly detailed, shot on 35mm lens, photorealistic.
